findings.evidence: The proof already starts from Mathlib's coefficient-general lemma: `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Gaussian/GaussianIntegral.lean:114: theorem integrable_rpow_mul_exp_neg_mul_sq {b : ℝ} (hb : 0 < b) ...`; the PR specializes it at `HermiteGaussian.lean:77` with `b := (1 : ℝ) / 2` and exports only the specialized result at lines 87-88.
findings.fix: Prove the general form first, e.g. `integrable_eval_mul_exp_neg_mul_sq {b : ℝ} (hb : 0 < b) (p : ℝ[X]) : Integrable (fun x => p.eval x * Real.exp (-b * x ^ 2))`, and derive the current half-normal and `ℤ[X]` wrappers from it.
findings.issue: `integrable_eval_mul_gaussian` hard-codes `Real.exp (-(x ^ 2 / 2))`, losing the natural positive-coefficient Gaussian-polynomial integrability API.

summary: One exported integrability theorem is fixed to the standard-normal coefficient even though the proof and surrounding Mathlib API naturally support any positive Gaussian coefficient.
